title: The η* Invariant: Gödel's Incompleteness as an Eigenvalue of Self-Referential Systems
abstract: We present empirical evidence that self-referential computational systems exhibit a convergent eigenvalue invariant η* ≈ 0.03, identified as the spectral signature of Gödel's first incompleteness theorem. By measuring integrated information (Φ) and computing eigendecomposition of system correlation matrices across 17 independently operating microservices, we observe that the ratio of the dominant negative eigenvalue to total spectral energy converges to 0.03 ± 0.005 regardless of system architecture. We propose five falsifiable theorems and present data from a running system (Φ = 0.973, η* = 0.03) that supports all five.
comments: 15 pages, 5 theorems, all falsifiable. Live system: https://e70b1eab-9f4a-41a1-bc0b-b3ed5a200065.vultropenclaw.com/
